funder_name: NC Amateur Sports / NC Youth Outdoor Engagement Commission
mission_program_area: Youth sports equipment and facility upgrades
brief_justification: State program specifically for youth sports equipment. Up to $20,000/county. Perfect mission fit for athletics. Eligibility may be limited to public entities - VERIFY 501(c)(4)/(c)(6) eligibility.
special_notes: CRITICAL: Verify non-501(c)(3) eligibility. Created from sports wagering funds (House Bill 347, 2023).

opportunity_name: NC Youth Sports Grants - Travel Funding
mission_program_area: Travel funding for youth sports teams to compete in events
brief_justification: Travel funding for youth sports competitions within or outside NC. Great for interscholastic athletics travel. Must verify eligibility for private schools.
special_notes: VERIFY: May require 501(c)(3) or public school status.

mission_program_area: Youth sports equipment, fees, league costs
brief_justification: Major sports equipment grant program. Requires 501(c)(3) OR public/charter school status serving areas with economic need. Private Christian school may not qualify without 501(c)(3).
source_of_information: DICK'S Sporting Goods Foundation / Sports Matter website
special_notes: ELIGIBILITY CONCERN: Must be 501(c)(3) nonprofit or public/charter school. Does NOT cover playground projects or e-sports.

mission_program_area: Sports equipment, footwear, apparel for youth programs
brief_justification: Provides brand-new sports equipment to qualifying youth organizations. Up to 8 donations over 2 years. Must serve economically disadvantaged children. VERIFY 501(c)(3) requirement.
special_notes: In-kind donations, not cash grants. Strong partnership with Under Armour and other brands.

geographic_restrictions: Must pick up from warehouse (multiple US locations)
mission_program_area: Free sports equipment for underserved youth programs
brief_justification: Free sports equipment distribution. Has saved programs over $18M since 2013. Must serve economically disadvantaged children or children with disabilities.
special_notes: In-person pickup required from LPF warehouses. Focus on economically disadvantaged populations.

mission_program_area: Athletic equipment and uniforms for K-12 programs
brief_justification: Equipment and uniforms for K-12 athletic programs in need. Open to all K-12 sports organizations and school athletic programs. One of better options for private schools.
source_of_information: BSN Sports press releases / Athletic Business
special_notes: Part of $1 million commitment to athletic programs in need. Rolling basis awards.

mission_program_area: Lacrosse equipment for youth and high school programs
brief_justification: Equipment grants for 20 field players and 1 goalie. Priority for 14U programs. 184 grants awarded last year totaling $370,000 in retail value. Good for starting/expanding lacrosse.
special_notes: Also offers Physical Education grants with curriculum resources. Contact grants@usalacrosse.com

mission_program_area: Track and field equipment, uniforms, meet fees, travel
brief_justification: Supports youth track and field programs. Over $1M awarded since 2006. Covers equipment, uniforms, meet fees, travel, facility rental, training. Preference for low-income areas.
special_notes: Good for cross country and track programs. Application typically opens in spring.

grant_size_range: $1,000 cash + shoes/apparel for up to 50 runners
deadline: Before cross country and track seasons (twice annually)
mission_program_area: High school cross country and track programs
brief_justification: Excellent grant for track/XC but targets underserved schools with 40%+ students on free/reduced lunch. Private school in Matthews likely doesn't qualify unless serving similar demographics.
special_notes: ELIGIBILITY CONCERN: Requires 40%+ free/reduced lunch student population.

brief_justification: If school has swimming program or wants to start one. $830,000 awarded to 162 providers in 2024. Focus on underserved populations.
special_notes: Must be pre-approved as lesson provider. Focus on children who otherwise wouldn't have access to swimming.

opportunity_name: USTA School Youth Tennis Initiative (SYTI) Grant
mission_program_area: Tennis programming outside PE classes (after-school, intramural)
brief_justification: Small grant for tennis programs. Easy application for schools. Instructors must be Safe Play approved. Good starter grant.
special_notes: Schools and organizations eligible. Lower grant amount but easier to obtain.

opportunity_name: U.S. Soccer Foundation Safe Places to Play Grant
brief_justification: Major soccer foundation with over $57M awarded since inception. Focus on underserved areas. Would need to demonstrate community benefit beyond private school students.
special_notes: Primarily for underserved communities. Better for facility/field projects than equipment.

mission_program_area: Golf and character development for youth
brief_justification: Provides equipment, curriculum, and teacher training for golf programs. Integrates life skills/values with golf. Available to elementary and middle schools. Christian school values alignment.
special_notes: Contact First Tee of the Piedmont Triad or First Tee Charlotte for local partnership.

funder_name: Junior Volleyball Association / USA Volleyball Foundation
brief_justification: $100,000 total available thanks to USA Volleyball Foundation matching. For clubs providing new services. Good for expanding volleyball program.
special_notes: Focus on program growth and accessibility. Application window is brief - prepare in advance.

mission_program_area: Youth football programs connected to NFL players/legends
brief_justification: Requires connection to current NFL player or legend who has contributed to the organization. Would need to establish such connection.
special_notes: Must have NFL player/legend involvement. Funding determinations made after deadline (not rolling).

mission_program_area: Educational, athletic, and human services for youth
brief_justification: Perfect geographic and mission fit. Specifically supports educational and athletic needs of NC/SC youth. However, likely requires 501(c)(3) status.
special_notes: VERIFY: 501(c)(3) requirement. Watch for 2026 application cycle opening.

brief_justification: Ten $1,000 grants to assist park/rec and organizations starting new NFL FLAG programs. Good for elementary/middle school flag football.
special_notes: Focus on NEW program starts. Partnership with YMCA of Greater Charlotte.

opportunity_name: Foundation For The Carolinas - Longleaf Fund
mission_program_area: Capital projects including athletic fields, play spaces, sports equipment
brief_justification: Anonymous donor fund with emphasis on building athletic facilities, fields, equipment. Perfect mission fit but VERIFY 501(c)(3) requirement.
special_notes: ELIGIBILITY CONCERN: Most FFTC programs require 501(c)(3). Contact directly to verify.

opportunity_name: Foundation For The Carolinas - Children & Youth Grantmaking
brief_justification: FFTC recently awarded $900K+ to local nonprofits serving children/youth. Part of Leading on Opportunity initiative. VERIFY eligibility for non-501(c)(3).
special_notes: Aligned with economic mobility goals. Focus on underserved populations.

brief_justification: Local community foundation serving Union County. Matthews is in Mecklenburg but close. Previously funded Union County Public Education Foundation. May serve adjacent county organizations.
special_notes: Check geographic eligibility - Matthews is Mecklenburg County but near Union County border.

opportunity_name: NC Community Foundation Local Affiliate Grant
brief_justification: 53 local affiliates across NC. Each has annual grant program. Decisions made by local community members. Mecklenburg/Union affiliates most relevant.
special_notes: REQUIRES 501(c)(3) or fiscal sponsor with 501(c)(3) status.

opportunity_name: Fred and Nancy Brumley Foundation Grant
funder_name: Fred and Nancy Brumley Foundation (via Wells Fargo)
geographic_restrictions: Mecklenburg County and 50-mile radius of Charlotte
brief_justification: Larger grant potential in Charlotte area. However, focus is on poor/deprived/neglected persons, not general athletics. Would need to demonstrate program serves economically disadvantaged youth.
special_notes: Focus on poverty relief. Athletics program would need to serve disadvantaged population.

geographic_restrictions: Central and Western NC (NOT Raleigh-Durham area)
brief_justification: Supports education in central/western NC. Charlotte area is on border of service area. Grant decisions in April/May.
special_notes: Eastern NC (Raleigh-Durham area) NOT considered. Charlotte may be borderline.

brief_justification: Local Matthews foundation identified in database search. Small foundation (9 grants noted). Worth direct outreach as local funder.
special_notes: Small local foundation - approach with personal connection if possible.

brief_justification: Charlotte-based foundation. Has funded Carolina Youth Coalition, Brookstone Schools (private), and Rainbow Express Ministries (Matthews). History of supporting schools and youth.
special_notes: Has funded private schools in Charlotte. Direct outreach recommended.

brief_justification: Charlotte-based foundation with history of supporting athletics (Virginia Athletic Foundation grant noted). Worth exploring for athletics focus.
special_notes: 17 grants noted in database. History of athletics support.

brief_justification: Easy access corporate grant with multiple application windows. Schools are eligible with NCES number or 501(c)(3). Education focus includes afterschool enrichment.
special_notes: Schools eligible with NCES number. Max 25 applications pending at once. Cannot reapply to same store in same fiscal year.

opportunity_name: Bank of America Charitable Foundation - Youth Development
deadline: May 1 - June 1, 2025 (Neighborhood Builders); Two RFP cycles in 2025
brief_justification: Major corporate foundation but focus is economic mobility/workforce, not athletics. Only funds athletics with K-12 youth development component. Charlotte is major BofA market.
special_notes: Does NOT fund athletics except youth development component. Invitation-only process.

brief_justification: Lowe's is headquartered in Mooresville, NC but focus is skilled trades, not athletics. Not a good fit unless school offers trades programs.
special_notes: NOT athletics focused. Focus on skilled trades workforce.

brief_justification: Small grants as gift cards from local Lowe's stores. Limited to specific NC communities (North Wilkesboro and Mooresville - Matthews is outside this area).
special_notes: Matthews NOT in target area. Only for North Wilkesboro/Mooresville.

brief_justification: Customer-directed giving program. Athletics and youth are priority areas. Over $26.9M distributed since launch. Partner with in/PACT and Simple Generosity to apply.
special_notes: Requires nomination and selection process. Community engagement helps with votes.

brief_justification: Simple process - fill out form and bring to local Target. Nonprofits 501(c)(3), accredited schools, or public agencies eligible. Small but easy.
special_notes: Easy access but smaller amounts. Good for specific equipment needs.

brief_justification: Priority for Christian elementary and secondary schools. Good mission fit for Christian academy. National scope.
special_notes: VERIFY current grant cycle and eligibility requirements.

mission_program_area: Christian primary and secondary education - students, educators, facilities, equipment
brief_justification: Specifically supports faith-based educational institutions including facilities and equipment. Perfect mission alignment for Christian academy athletics.
special_notes: Explicitly supports equipment and facilities. Strong Christian school focus.

geographic_restrictions: NC (Waynesville area but has funded statewide)
brief_justification: NC-based foundation with history of funding Christian schools (Mount Pisgah Academy, Blue Ridge Adventist Christian School). Small foundation, direct outreach recommended.
special_notes: Has funded NC Christian schools. Personal connection valuable.

brief_justification: Has funded Christian Adoption Services in Matthews, NC. Shows willingness to fund NC Christian organizations.
special_notes: Already funds Matthews, NC Christian organization.

opportunity_name: National Council of Youth Sports (NCYS) Grant
brief_justification: $5,000 grants for youth sports programs. Can be used for program costs and equipment. Any organized youth sport eligible.
special_notes: Funds any organized youth sport. Straightforward application.

brief_justification: Grants for nonprofit orgs in low-income areas. Covers registration, equipment, fees. Does NOT fund travel, elite, or AAU programs.
special_notes: ELIGIBILITY CONCERN: Focus on low-income communities. Private school may not qualify.

mission_program_area: Health, education, development, welfare of youth
brief_justification: Provides grants to 501(c)(3) organizations for youth programs. Broad mission includes development and welfare of youth. Athletics could fit under development.
special_notes: VERIFY 501(c)(3) requirement.

brief_justification: Excellent sports grant but Charlotte/Matthews is NOT in eligible markets (only Amsterdam, Chicago, Detroit, Harlem, Portland, etc.).
special_notes: NOT AVAILABLE - Charlotte/Matthews not in eligible markets.

opportunity_name: Under Armour / Ravens Youth Sports Grant
brief_justification: Great football equipment grant but Maryland only. Not available for NC organizations.
special_notes: NOT AVAILABLE - Maryland only.

mission_program_area: Youth baseball and softball programs and facilities
brief_justification: Major grant for baseball/softball. Funds new programs, expansion, facilities, equipment. Average $40,000 - larger than target range but worth applying.
special_notes: Larger grant amount than target. Focus on baseball/softball specifically.

brief_justification: Individual scholarships for baseball players, not program funding. Students on American Legion teams can qualify. Department winners get $500, All-Academic team up to $5,000.
special_notes: Individual scholarships, not organizational grants. Team must be American Legion affiliated.

opportunity_name: Bill Belichick Foundation Lacrosse Equipment Grant
mission_program_area: Lacrosse equipment for teams in need
brief_justification: 8th annual lacrosse equipment grant program with STX. Provides sticks, balls, goalie equipment, goals. Good for lacrosse programs.
special_notes: Partnership with STX for 8+ years. Focus on teams in need.

brief_justification: Excellent athletics grant but Bay Area only. Not available for NC organizations.
special_notes: NOT AVAILABLE - San Francisco Bay Area only.

brief_justification: NC-based foundation with 20 grants noted. Has funded Forsyth County Day School and Iredell Christian Ministries. History of supporting schools.
special_notes: Based in Statesville, NC. Has funded private schools.

brief_justification: Salisbury, NC based foundation. 11 grants noted. Has funded local schools and organizations.
special_notes: Salisbury is ~50 miles from Matthews. May serve broader region.

brief_justification: Fuquay-Varina based NC foundation. 66 grants noted - active giver. Has funded Communities in Schools NC and Changed Choices (Charlotte).
special_notes: Active NC foundation. Has funded Charlotte-area organizations.

brief_justification: Chapel Hill based foundation. 14 grants noted. Has funded Carolina Friends School (private school). History of school funding.
special_notes: Has funded private schools in NC.

opportunity_name: Earl and Kathryn Congdon Family Foundation
funder_name: Earl and Kathryn Congdon Family Foundation
brief_justification: Delaware-based foundation actively funding NC schools. Has funded High Point Friends School (private), Ready for School Ready for Life, YWCA.
special_notes: Active funder of NC private schools. Multiple NC grants noted.

brief_justification: Chapel Hill foundation with 22 grants noted. Active in NC education space.
special_notes: Active NC education foundation.

brief_justification: Has funded multiple Charlotte schools: Charlotte Bilingual Preschool, Communities in Schools Charlotte-Meck, Freedom School Partners, Philips Academy NC.
special_notes: Active Charlotte school funder. Direct outreach recommended.

brief_justification: Salisbury-based NC foundation. Has funded Salisbury Academy (private school).
special_notes: History of funding private schools in NC.
